title: English-learning story (CEFR graded reader)
description: Machine-read source of truth for one graded-reader lesson story, at projects/{slug}/learn/{lang}/{slug}.lesson.json. The story text + the controlled-vocabulary contract live here; a learner-facing page (story + objective + glossary) is rendered into a sibling .md. The deterministic gate (scripts/check-graded-reader.mjs) enforces the reproducible grading facts (grounded in docs/research/2026-06-06-cefr-graded-reader.md): per-CEFR-level sentence-length cap, story word-count band, every target word repeated >= min, and an undeclared-beyond-core word budget. Whether the story is pedagogically good / culturally apt is content-review's job, not the gate's.

cefr_level.description: CEFR level. Drives the gate's default sentence-length cap, word-count band, and beyond-core budget.

learning_objective.description: What the learner should be able to do/understand after this story (can-do statement).

story.description: The graded-reader text itself (paragraphs separated by blank lines). This is what the gate tokenizes.

target_vocabulary.description: The new words this lesson teaches. Each MUST appear in the story >= min_target_repetitions times (you can't teach a word you don't use).

word.description: The headword (base form). Matched inflection-aware in the story.

gloss.description: A learner-friendly definition (ideally itself within level).

example.description: Optional example sentence for the glossary.

allowed_extra.description: Project-specific extra headwords to treat as controlled beyond the shipped core (e.g. a theme's common words). Lowercase base forms.

min_words.description: Override the level's story minimum word count.

max_words.description: Override the level's story maximum word count.

min_target_repetitions.description: Override how many times each target word must appear. Default 2 (gate).

max_uncontrolled_ratio.description: Override the beyond-core word budget (fraction of total words). Default is per-level (A1 0.10 .. C2 0.80).
